def: s.f. A woman; a wife; a mother; the wife of a son or brother's son, a daughter-in-law; a maid-servant; — birth, production; birthplace.
s.f. Side; part; outward or adjacent part, quarter, tract, or region; party; direction; — postpn. In the direction (of, -kī), towards
more: n. f. جنب the side. See اور 1, 3, 4. jānib , adv. Towards (Com. taraf ). jānib-dār , adj. Partial; biassed. jānib-dār , n. m. A supporter; second; patron; partizan. jānib-dārī , n. f. Partiality; bias. jānib-dārī k . v. a. To be biassed in favor of; to take one's part; espouse a side. jānib se , adv. In the name of; in behalf of.
s. f. Part, side. adv. Towards. جانب دار jānib-dār, A supporter, a second. جانب داري jānib-dār-ī, Supporting, seconding.
subs: adj. & s.m. Taking a side, partial; — a supporter, second; a patron; a partisan
s.f. Supporting, seconding; partiality, favour ( = t̤araf-dārī); — jānib-dārī karnā (-kī), To take the side or part (of), to support, second; to show partiality (to), to be biassed in favour (of)
adv. From; in the name (of), in behalf (of)
s.m. This person, the writer (of a letter, — used by a superior in addressing a subordinate).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جانِب, from Arabic جَانِب (jānib).

def: s.m. Support, assistance, seconding, countenancing; partiality.
more: (from جنب) s. m. Support, seconding, countenancing, assistance, partiality.

def: adj. & s.m. On both sides, mutual; — both sides or parties; the contending parties (in a suit or case)
more: n. m. Dual. Both sides or parties; the parties in a case. īṅ jānib , n. m. The writer (a superior addressing a subordinate).
s. dual (of جانب) Mutual, on both sides. جانبين سي jānibain se, adv. Reciprocally, alternately, mutually.
subs: adv. From both sides or parties; mutually, reciprocally; alternately.

def: s.f. A she-camel that one gives or lends to people, with money, in order that they may bring corn or other provision for him.
more: s. A she camel given (or intrusted) to a people that they may procure you provisions on her.

def: s.m. An expression having more than one meaning, a pun, a double-entendre.
more: s. m. Double-entendre, an expression having more than one meaning.

def: s.m. An embryo, a fœtus; the child, or young, in the womb.
more: n. m. Fœtus; embryo; an unborn child.

def: s.m. A father, progenitor.
ex: میرا اور ان کا گوشت پوست ایک ھے، میں جنتا کا بیٹا ھوں، خادم ھوں !
Alexander Fadeyev
My flesh and skin and their [flesh and skin] is one, I am a son of the people, a servant!
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit जनता (janatā), from जन् (jan), from Proto-Indo-European *ǵenh₁-. Compare Spanish gente.

def: part. Engendered, begotten, born, produced, occasioned.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَنَّت (jannat), borrowed from Arabic جَنَّة (janna). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi جنت (jnt /⁠janat⁠/).

def: s.m. A father, &c. = janitā.
ex:
دُکھوں کی گِنْتِی سے کالی ہَے زِنْدَگی کی کِتاب
تو جَنْتَری میں اُنْھیں سال و ماہ لِکھ لِیجے
dukhõ kī gintī se kālī hai zindagī kī kitāb
to jantarī mẽ unhẽ sāl o māh likh līje

The book of life is dark from the counting of sorrows
So write them in the calendar by year and month

def: s.f. Progenitrix, mother.
ex:
دُکھوں کی گِنْتِی سے کالی ہَے زِنْدَگی کی کِتاب
تو جَنْتَری میں اُنْھیں سال و ماہ لِکھ لِیجے
dukhõ kī gintī se kālī hai zindagī kī kitāb
to jantarī mẽ unhẽ sāl o māh likh līje

The book of life is dark from the counting of sorrows
So write them in the calendar by year and month

def: s.m. Entanglements, embarrassments, troubles, cares or anxieties (of wordly concerns or engagements); trouble, complication, bother, fuss; a teasing, tiring, or troublesome person or business; an encumbrance, a clog, load; a plague, pest, bore
more: n. m. H. jan man, jāl net. Plague; mess. See uljhāo and bakheṛā , 1—3., and āljanjāl . Phr. A peck of troubles; a sea of troubles. 1. Bāl janjāl, bāl hī siṅghār . Prov. Hair is a plague and an ornament too. 2. Zulf hai chehre pe, yā jaṅjāl hai, Jumbish-i-abrū hai, yā bhauṅchāl hai? Those raven locks heart's plague but make, That moving brow's the heart's earthquake. janjāl meṅ phaṅsnā , v. n. To get into, or be surrounded by difficulties, etc.; to get into a mess.
s. m. Trouble, difficulty, plague, embarrassment, perplexity.
(corrupt. of جزايل q. v. or perhaps from s. جنجال) s. A swivel, &c.
subs: (-ke), To get into an entanglement, fall into difficulties; to get into a mess.

def: s.m. A large musket, a swivel ( = jazāʼil, q.v.). s.m. A large musket or rifle (used with a prong or rest); a wall-piece; a swivel
subs: s.m. One who fires a wall-piece or swivel.

def: adj. & s.m. Occasioning trouble, embarrassing, troublesome, &c.; — a troublesome person; one who molests.
s. m. Man and beast.
adj. & s.m. See s.v. jān, 'knowledge.' adj. (met.) Dear, beloved
subs: s.m. Animator, creator of life (an epithet of the Deity)
jān-bāz, adj. & s.m. Sporting with life, risking life, venturesome, daring, intrepid, spirited, mettlesome; — one who sports with life, a daring or intrepid man, &c.

s.f. The state of life being at stake (e.g. jān-bāzī-ke waqt, 'when life is at stake'); risking life (in battle, &c.); intrepidity, mettle, spiritedness; working (at anything) as if life depended on success
To risk life (in battle, or an enterprise, &c.); to act bravely or fearlessly, be intrepid, &c.
(-kī, or apnī), To save the life (of); to shirk, skulk; to funk
'The soul to be surrendered to God'; to die, expire
adj. & s.m. Animating, life-giving; forgiving; — forgiver of a capital crime; pardoner
s.f. Giving life, vivification; sparing life; forgiveness, pardon (of a capital crime); salvation
To give up the ghost, to expire
adj. At the point of death, dying, expiring
(-par), To feel life to be a burden, to be weary of life
v.n. To be exposed to imminent danger
v.n. To be in danger of life
(or -pa) khelnā, v.n. To put life at stake, to risk life, to run into danger, to place oneself in a perilous position; to sport or trifle with (one's) life
(-meṅ), To receive the breath of life, to be quickened; to receive new life, be refreshed, to revive
s.f. Risk or danger of life; — adv. In danger
jān ćhupānā (apnī), To skrink (from), escape (from), to skulk, shirk ( = jān baćānā)
(-kī, or apnī), To escape with life; to get rid of
adj. Having life or strength; powerful, active; lively, spirited (as a horse); — s.m. A living thing, an animal (generally implying strength); — jān-dārī, s.f. Animation, spirit, spiritedness
(-par), To yield up life; to lose or sacrifice (one's) life (for), to die (for)
(or supār), adj. Resigning (one's) life into the hands of another (an epithet of a lover)
adj. Life destroying, destructive of life (as poison, or a snake, &c.)
s.f. The taking or destruction of life
adj. Life-consuming, soul-tormenting, very painful; heart-inflaming; beloved; — jān-se jānā, v.n. To give up life, to pass away, to die
v.t. To deprive of life, to kill
v.n. To wash one's hands of life, to be weary of life; to despair of life

adj. Ready to sacrifice oneself for another, devoted, zealous, fervent; — jān-fishānī, s.f. Sacrificing (one's) life in the service of another, devotion, devotedness, zeal; hard labour; extreme diligence; — jān-fishānī karnā, v.n. To be very devoted or zealous; to practise extreme diligence; to labour or strive hard, to strain every nerve
jān-kā lāgū, jān-kā lewā, s.m. Deadly foe, mortal enemy; — jān-kā lāgū honā (-kī), To pursue (one) to death; to persecute, harass, worry
adj. Life-reducing, soul-exhausting; heart-breaking, afflicting; affecting, pathetic; — jān-kāhī, s.f. Diminution of life; what tends to shorten life
s.m., jān-kandanī, s.f., jān-kanī, s.f. The agonies of death, agony, torture
(-kī), To reduce to straits, to overcome, exhaust; to worry, tease, vex, annoy, plague, torment
(-ke sabab), To sacrifice or give up life (for)
s.f. Safety of (one's) life; life, quarter
(-ko), To hold (one) as dear as life, to deem precious, to value or prize highly
adj. Soul-melting, life-dissolving, life-destroying; baneful, pernicious; weakening; consuming; — jān-gudāzī, s.f. Dissolving or taking away of life; banefulness, &c.
(-kī), To destroy life, to kill; to worry, tease, &c. ( = jān khānā); to exert oneself to the utmost, strain every nerve
(-kī, merī), To be revived ( = jān paṛnā); to be satisfied, be comforted
adj. Scattering life; ready to sacrifice life (for another), devoted; dying in agony; — jān-nis̤ārī, s.f. Readiness to sacrifice life (for another), devotedness, devotion
v.n. Life to depart, to die
s.f. lit. 'Clinging to life'; a sudden reviving of consciousness and life in a dying man previous to his giving up the ghost (caused by the weeping and lamentation of those around him)
v.n. To make life wearisome; to worry; to work to death (used by women)
adj. Lifeless, dead; dispirited; listless; weak, feeble, inactive.

def: adv. = jān-ke, and jān-būjh-ke, qq.v. adv. Knowing, wittingly, &c. = jān-būjh-ke, q.v.
subs: v.n. To pretend or feign ignorance.

def: s.m. = jākaṛ, q.v.
more: s. m. Money (or other pledge) left for a thing purchased, while it is carried away to be finally approved.

def: adv. Knowing, wittingly, &c. = jān-būjh-ke, q.v.
subs: v.n. To pretend or feign ignorance.

def: s.m. Birth, production, origin; nativity; existence, life; state of existence; life-time
more: n. m. S. जन्मन् birth, Pr. jammo . 1. Birth; nativity. 2. Life; existence; age. 1. Janam ke dukhyā, nām Chain-sukh! Prov. From birth doomed to pain, Fortunatus his name! (Said when penury at home affects an appearance of wealth abroad). 2. Kar khetī par-des ko jāë, tā ko janam akārath jāë . Prov. Who till the soil and leave for a foreign land, But throw away the life at their command. 3. Gur to aisā chāhiye jūṅ siklī-gar hoë, Janam janam kā morchhā chhin meṅ ḍāle khoë . Dohā. A true priest as skilled polisher should be, From life-long rust the soul set instant free. 4. Sās, nanad morī janam kī bairan, Charchā kareṅgī das meṅ, jīrā parāë bas meṅ . Song. Ṭhumrī . My husband's odious people are my life-long enemies, Where ten men are my faults they tell—my heart another's is. 5. Janam na dekhā boryā, supne āī khāt . Prov. He never saw a mat in his life and he dreams of abed! janam-andhā , n…

s. m. 1. Birth. 2. Life. جنماشٿمي जन्माष्टमी janmāsh̤ṭamī, The eighth of Bhādra-kr̤ish̤ṇa-paksh̤, the birthday of Kr̤ish̤ṇa. جنم بهوم janam-bhūm ( जन्मभूमि ) s. f. Birth-place. جنم پتري janam-patrī ( जन्मपत्री ) s. f. Horoscope, nativity. جنم پتري کي بده ملنا janm-patrī kī bidhi milnā, To meet oneʼs fate. جنم جلا janam-jalā, adj. Burned or spoiled from its birth, originally bad. جنم دن janam-din ( जन्मदिन ) s. m. Birthday. جنم روگي जन्मरोगी janam-rogī, adj. Valetudinarian. جنم سوارتهـ जन्मस्वार्थ janam-swārth, A fortunate or wellspent life. جنم مرن जन्ममरण janam-maraṇ, Eternal death.
subs: vulg. janam-ashṭamī (S. janma+ash˚), s.f. The eighth day in the dark half of the month Sāvan-Bhādoṅ, the birth-day of Kr̤ishṇa
(janma+an˚), s.m. Another birth; another state of existence; the future life; regeneration
adj. Belonging to or done in another life; of or pertaining to another state of existence
vulg. janam-andhā, adj. & s.m. Blind from birth; — one born blind
(apnā), To waste (one's) life; to indulge in sensual and sinful pleasures
To be ruined for life; to become a widow
s.m. & adv. A whole life, a life-time; — through life, for a life-time
janm-bhūm, janm-bhūmī, s.f. Birth-place, native land, fatherland

janam-pattrā, s.m. = janam-pattrī, s.f. Horoscope, astrological record of nativity, the paper or scroll on which are recorded the year, lunar day, configuration, and relative position of the planets, &c. of the birth of a particular individual, and a table of his fortunes throughout life
To receive (one's) destined award, to meet (one's) fate
adj. (f. -ī), 'Burned or spoiled from birth,' originally bad; unfortunate, wretched; — very little
janm-ā-janam, adv. From birth to birth, one life after another; in successive transmigrations
s.m. = janma-da, janmad, s.m. 'Birth-giver'; father, progenitor; — the Author of existence
adj. & s.m. Sickly from birth; — a valetudinarian
s.m. A fortunate or well-spent life
s.f. A short horoscope; a generalogical table
s.m. 'Pillar or stay of birth'; an epithet of Vishnu
janam-lagan, s.m. The conjunction of the sun and a sign of the Ecliptic at the moment of a birth; the moment of birth; a horoscope, &c. ( = janam-pattrī)
v.n. To become incarnate (as a deity); to be born; to transmigrate (a soul)
(janma+ut˚), s.m. A festival held on the anniversary of Kr̤ishṇa's birthday.
ex: جَدِید نَثْر اُرْدُو کا جَنَم فورْٹ وِلّیَم کالِج کَلْکَتَّہ میں ہُوا
jadīd nasr urdū kā janam forṭ villyam kālij kalkatta mẽ huā
The birth of modern Urdu prose occurred in Fort William College, Calcutta

To receive (one's) destined award, to meet (one's) fate

ety: S. जन्म+ā˚ = āw = Prk. आव or आवे = S. आपय˚+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: v.t. (caus. of janamnā), To beget; to cause to vegetate.
more: v. a. 1. To beget. 2. To make vegetate.

def: s.m. = janwāsā, q.v. s.m. The place at the bride's house where the bridegroom and his train are received.
n. prop. The son and successor of Rājā Pariksh̤it.
s.m. A living being, a man, a creature, an animal; — adj. Innate, inborn; constitutional, inherited, natural; — impotent from birth.
more: adj. 1. Constitutional; inherited; natural. 2. Inborn; inbred; ingrained. 3. Impotent from birth ( nā-mard ).

def: s.m. = janmī, q.v. s.m. A living being, a man, a creature, an animal; — adj. Innate, inborn; constitutional, inherited, natural; — impotent from birth.
s.m. See s.v. janm.
more: s. m. A festival in commemoration of the birth of Krish̤ṇa.

def: s.m. The father of the jinn or genii; the genii (collectively), the race of the jinn; a jinn.
more: s. m. (collect. of جنّ) The race of genii or demons. (Pers. jān ).

ety: jān˚ = Prk. जाण(इ) = S. जाना(ति), rt. ज्ञा;+nā = Prk. अणअं = S. अनीयं
def: s.m. Knowledge, cognizance, apprehension, opinion, &c. (e.g. hamāre jānne-meṅ āʼī hai)
more: v. a. S. ज्ञानं from ज्ञा to know. Pr. janai , Pāli. jānāti he knows. To know; know again; recognize. Jān māre bānyā, pahchhān māre chor . Prov. The banya sells his dearest friend, the thief robs all whom he hath kenned. jāne, jane, na jāne, na jānye , adv. 1. ( jāne, jane ). As if; as though; that is to say; suppose. 2. Who knows? ( kaun jāne ); God knows! ( wohī jāne ). jān lenā , v. n. To get a knowledge of; to understand.
v. a. To know, understand, comprehend, suppose, believe, deem, think, conceive, judge, account, esteem, fancy, to trust.
subs: (-ko), To become known or clear, to be evident; to be perceived, to be visible, to appear; to seem, to occur (mentally, to); to approve itself (to)
(-ko), To bear in mind, to remember
v.t. To get a knowledge of, to understand, perceive, apprehend; to recognize.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀚𑀸𑀡 (jāṇa) + ـنا (-nā, verbal suffix), the first element of which is from Sanskrit ज्ञा (jñā). Cognate with Marathi जाणणे (jāṇṇe), Old English cnāwan (whence English know).

def: v.n. & t. = جننا jannā, q.v. s.m. Knowledge, cognizance, apprehension, opinion, &c. (e.g. hamāre jānne-meṅ āʼī hai)
more: v. n. To be delivered (of a child); to be born. 1. Jīb jane ek bār, māṅ jane bār bār . Wom. Prov. The tongue brings forth but once, the bearing-woman oft. (What is said once cannot be unsaid). 2. Naṅgī ho-ke kātā sūt, buḍdhī ho-ke jāyā pūt . Prov. She spins when she is naked, and bears when she is old. (If she had spun sooner she would not have been naked, if she had borne a son when she was young he would have supported her in her old age).

v. a. To bring forth; produce, or cast young; to generate; breed; beget. 1. Jannā aur marnā brābar hai . Wom. Prov. The throes of child birth are like the agonies of death. 2. Terī māṅ ne kyā khā-ke janā thā? Wom. What did your mother eat when she bore you? (Did she eat chillies that you are so flery?). 3. Jan jan-ke chhoṛ dīyā . Wom. I have given birth to many such. (I am fit to be your mother.). 4. Maiṅ ne kyā dard khā-ke nahīṅ janā thā? Wom. Suffered I no pain when I bare him? (Shall I not grieve for one who has cost me so much?)
subs: (-ko), To become known or clear, to be evident; to be perceived, to be visible, to appear; to seem, to occur (mentally, to); to approve itself (to)
(-ko), To bear in mind, to remember
v.t. To get a knowledge of, to understand, perceive, apprehend; to recognize.

ety: jan˚ = Prk. जण(इ) or जणे(इ) = S. जनय(ति), rt. जन्
def: v.n. To be born; to be delivered (of a child), to give birth (to); — v.t. To bring forth, produce; to give birth to; to cast young; to generate, beget, breed.
more: v. n. To produce young, to be delivered (of a child), to be born.

def: s.f. The garden, the abode of recompense, paradise
more: n. f. lit . a garden. Paradise; heaven. 1. Garmiyoṅ meṅ Kashmīr jannat hai . Prov. In the summer Kashmīr is Paradise. 2. Khudā unko jannat nasib kare! Mah.
s. f. The garden, paradise. جنت آشياني jannat-āshiyān-ī, Whose nest (abode) is paradise; the deceased or late (king, &c.). جنة البلاد jannatu-l-bilād, The garden of the region (an epithet of Bengal).
subs: jannat-āshiyānī, Whose place of rest is paradise; the deceased or late (king, &c.)
The paradise of regions, an epithet of Bengal
jannat-ě-naʻīm, The garden of delight, a paradise so called
jannat-naṣīb, Whose abode or lot is paradise.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَنَّت (jannat), borrowed from Arabic جَنَّة (janna). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi جنت (jnt /⁠janat⁠/).

def: s.f. pl. (of jannat), Gardens.
adj. Of paradise, heavenly.
more: adj. Of paradise; heavenly. Yeh jannatī log hain . These are from Heaven. (Said of the simple and unsuspecting).

def: v.t. (Braj) = jānnā.
more: v. a. (in Braj) To know, &c. See جاننا jānnā.

def: s.f. = جنني jananī, q.v. conj. (Old H.) = jāno, q.v. conj. You would suppose; it seems as if; understand, suppose; that is, videlicet, to wit; like as, as if, as.
more: n. f. S. जननी A mother; the actual mother of a child, in contradistinction to a step mother. Āṭh mahīne garabh meṅ rākhā jannī bojh-marī . Kabīr. Eight months she bore you in her womb weighed down. (Expostulation with an undutiful son).

ety: 2nd pers. pl. of aor. or conditional of jānnā
def: conj. You would suppose; it seems as if; understand, suppose; that is, videlicet, to wit; like as, as if, as.
more: Understand, suppose; that is, videlicet, to wit.

def: s.m. (dialec.) = janeʼū, q.v. s.m. The sacrificial or sacred thread worn by the Brahmanical order among Hindūs (over the left shoulder and hanging down diagonally across the body to the right hip); a (thread-like) flaw in a jewel (such being regarded as sacred).
conj. (prov.) = jāno, q.v. conj. You would suppose; it seems as if; understand, suppose; that is, videlicet, to wit; like as, as if, as.
more: s. m. The brahmanical thread. See جنيو janeʼū.

def: s.m. See s.v. jan. adj. (met.) Dear, beloved
subs: s.m. Animator, creator of life (an epithet of the Deity)
jān-bāz, adj. & s.m. Sporting with life, risking life, venturesome, daring, intrepid, spirited, mettlesome; — one who sports with life, a daring or intrepid man, &c.

s.f. The state of life being at stake (e.g. jān-bāzī-ke waqt, 'when life is at stake'); risking life (in battle, &c.); intrepidity, mettle, spiritedness; working (at anything) as if life depended on success
To risk life (in battle, or an enterprise, &c.); to act bravely or fearlessly, be intrepid, &c.

v. n. (dakh.) See جهانسنا jhāṅsnā, sign. 2.

wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian جَنَّت (jannat), borrowed from Arabic جَنَّة (janna). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi جنت (jnt /⁠janat⁠/).

def: s.f. = jāṅt or jāṅtā.
more: n. f. S. यंत्र 1. A stone hand-mill for grinding corn. Nai apnā jāṅt chhahū, tāṅ ankā jāṅt māṅ pīsi lābah . Tir. If you have n't a mill of your own, go and grind it in some one else's. 2. A press for pressing indigo into a compact mass after boiling. 3. E. A weight put on the power arm of a lever for raising water.
s. f. A wooden trough for raising water.

def: s.m. = jāta, jāt, q.v.; — s.f. = jātā, q.v. part. Born, brought into existence, brought forth, engendered, grown, produced; — s.m. Male issue, a son
subs: adj. Embodied, having assumed a shape or form; beautiful, brilliant, golden; — s.m. Gold
s.m. A ceremony at the birth of a child when the navel string is divided (it consists in touching the infant's tongue thrice with ghī after appropriate prayers; cf. ćhaṭī).

def: s.f. Knowledge, intelligence; skill.
s.m. An instrument for drawing wire.
more: s. m. An instrument for drawing wire.
ex: میرا اور ان کا گوشت پوست ایک ھے، میں جنتا کا بیٹا ھوں، خادم ھوں !
Alexander Fadeyev
My flesh and skin and their [flesh and skin] is one, I am a son of the people, a servant!
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit जनता (janatā), from जन् (jan), from Proto-Indo-European *ǵenh₁-. Compare Spanish gente.

def: s.f. = jāṅt or jāṅtā.
more: n. m. S. जन् to be born. Children; offspring. Jorū, na jāṅtā, Allah mīyāṅ se nātā . Prov. Nor wife, nor daughter, God relation sole.
1. A stone-mill for grinding corn, a handmill of two stones one over the other, 2. A pair of bellows. جانتاکل jāntā-kal, s. f. See جانت jānt.

more: v. n. To be squeezed, to be pressed.

def: s.m. An amulet, a charm.

more: n. f. A mother. Terī jantī par tallāq hai! Wom. May your mother be divorced (if you do not so)!

def: s.f. Birth, &c. = janat, q.v. adj. Knowing, understanding; wise.
s.m. The herb gentian.

s.m. An instrument, machine, engine, implement, apparatus, appliance; a musical instrument; a dial; an observatory; an astrological or magic diagram, a magic square; an amulet, a charm, a philter; juggling
more: n. m. S. यन्त्र 1. An instrument; machine; engine; apparatus. 2. An astrological or magic diagram; a magic square. 3. An amulet; a philter. Kāle ke kāṭhe kā jantar na mantar . Prov. The cobra's bite nor skill nor charm can cure. (There is no cure for tyranny). jantar mantar , n. m. 1. Juggling; conjuring; enchanting by figures and incantations; magical or mystical ceremonies, in which diagrams are drawn, and charms or prayers are repeated. 2. An observatory ( rasadgāh ).

s. m. 1. A machine in general, any instrument or apparatus. 2. A musical instrument. 3. A dial. 4. An observatory. 5. A diagram of a mystical nature, or astrological character. 6. An amulet. جنترمنتر jantr-mantr ( यंत्रमंत्र ) s. m. Juggling, conjuring, enchanting by figures and incantations.
subs: s.m. Juggling, conjuring, enchanting by figures and incantations; magical or mystical ceremonies in which diagrams are drawn and charms or prayers are repeated; — an observatory.

def: s.f. Constraint, compulsion; pain, anguish, affliction.
s.m. A conjurer, juggler, wizard.
more: n. f. 1. An instrument for drawing wire. 2. A calendar; an almanac. jantrī meṅ nikālnā, yā kheṅchnā , v. a. 1. To draw a wire; to attenuate. 2. To make straight; take the crookedness out of one.
s. m. 1. ( यंत्र ) An instrument for drawing wire. 2. ( यंत्री ) A conjurer, a juggler, a wizard.
ex:
دُکھوں کی گِنْتِی سے کالی ہَے زِنْدَگی کی کِتاب
تو جَنْتَری میں اُنْھیں سال و ماہ لِکھ لِیجے
dukhõ kī gintī se kālī hai zindagī kī kitāb
to jantarī mẽ unhẽ sāl o māh likh līje

The book of life is dark from the counting of sorrows
So write them in the calendar by year and month

def: s.f. A perforated plate of steel through which ingots of gold, silver, &c., are drawn into wire; — an almanac, a calendar
subs: or nikālnā, v.t. To draw out into wire; to attenuate; to take the crookedness out (of one), make (one) straight.

adj. (f. janūbīya), Southern.

def: conj. To wit; though, although (cf. jāno).
s. m. (dakh.) See زنار zunnār.
s.f. A midwife's fee.
more: n. m. S. जामातृ, Pr. jāmāuo, jāmāaram, jāmāareṇa, jamāaro, jāmāa , Pālī. jāmātā; P. dāmād . A son-in-law. 1. Bhale ke bhāī, aur bure ke jaṅvāī . Prov. A brother to the good, a son-in-law to the bad. (The son-in-law sits on his wife's relations who conciliate him for his wife's sake). 2. Rotī pīṭtī, bhūkī piyāsī maiṅ to tere ghar āī, Mār mār merī haḍḍiyāṅ toṛīṅ, maryo terā jaṅvāī! Chaubolā. Hungry and thirsty, weeping, wailing to your house I came, You've beat and beat and broken my bones— your son-in-law death claim! 3. Beṭī kā bhalā chāhe, to bol jaṅvāī Lāl kī jaë! Prov. Who wish the daughter well the praises of the son-in-law must tell! 4. Zabar-dast sab kā jaṅvāī . Prov. The strong man is every body's son-in-law. (His orders are obeyed). 5. Dhī mūī, jaṅvāī chor . Prov. The daughter dead, the son-in-law hath sped.

def: s.m. A son-in-law ( = jamāʼī).

def: s.m. Muttering prayers or telling beads, &c. ( = jap, q.v.).
more: n. m. S. जप to utter in a low voice. See جپ Jog jāp Har jādo kailanhī, Gaurī man muskāë, E Mahādeo Gaurī bhulāe līë jāë . Tir. song. Har worship paid, enchantment used, the fair one inward smiled, This Mahadeo the fair one hath beguiled.
s. m. Muttering of prayers, &c. See جپ jap. (A Hindū term).

def: s.m. Muttering prayers, repeating in a murmuring tone passages from scripture, charms, names of a deity, &c.; — counting silently the beads of a rosary, &c.; a muttered prayer or spell
more: n. m. S. जप् to repeat in silence. Counting silently the beads of a rosary; repeating charms, the epithets of a god, or passages from the Shāstars. jap tap , n. m. Religious service; worship. jap karānā , v. a. To get a Brahman to count his beads for a sick person.

s. m. Muttering of prayers, repeating inaudibly passages from the scriptures, charms or names of a deity, counting silently the beads of a rosary, &c. جپ تپ jap tap ( जप and तपः ) s. m. Devotion, adoration. جپمال يا مالا jap-māl or -mālā ( जपमाला ) s. f. A rosary.
subs: adj. & s.m. Devoted to repetition of prayers, engaged in muttering prayers; — one so devoted, or so engaged, a saint-sage
(-se), To get a Brahman to repeat prayers, &c., or to count his beads (for a sick person)

def: s.m. Child-birth, delivery, accouchement.
more: n. m. S. जन to be born. Delivery; accouchement .

def: s.f. The China rose ( = javā); the flower of the plant hurhur, or Cleome viscosa.

more: n. m. One who counts his beads while he repeats some prayer. Ī bar jāpak bhelā hai, nenā kanait achhi, nenā dhartā se nāhīṅ . Tir. Wom. (The wife speaks at her husband). He is so taken up with his grand religious rites, while here is the child crying and he cannot take him in his arms for a moment!
s. m. One who counts his beads, or who recites to himself.

def: s.m. The causing time to pass away, passing or spending time.

more: s. (dakh.) Nutmeg ( Harris ): see جاي پهل jāʼe-phal.

def: s.m. (dialec. & prov.) = jāʼe-phal, q.v. s.m. Nutmeg, Myristica moschata.

more: s. m. One who counts his beads, or who recites to himself.

def: adj. & s.m. Uttering prayers in a low voice; — one who mutters prayers, &c. (see jap)

more: s. f. Flattery. See چاپلوسي chāplūs-ī.

ety: jap˚ = Prk. जंप(इ) = S. जल्प(ति), rt. जल्प्
def: v.t. To mutter or whisper (prayers or spells), to count (one's beads), to repeat (the name of any deity) inaudibly or mentally, to recite (the bead-roll), to make mention of.
more: v. a. To count one's beads; repeat the different epithets of a god. 1. Tulsī jape to Rām jap, aur nām mat le, Rām nām samser hai Jam ke sar meṅ de . Tulsi Dās. Tulsī, Ram's name take oft, no other name your tongue should say, Ram's name a sharp sword is wherewith the king of Hell to slay. 2. Zāmin, dunyā pāp hai, tiryā hai mahā pāp, Donoṅ ko tū phūṅk de, nām niranjan jāp . Zāmin. This world, O Zāmin's full of sin, and women worst of all. Burn them in fire and perish both, on His name only call.
v. a. To count oneʼs beads, to repeat the name of God internally, to recite the bead-roll, to make mention. جپت japat, part. Repeating prayers, worshipping.
v. (dakh.) To wait, expect, lie in wait for.

def: v.t. (Braj.) = japnā, q.v. v.t. To mutter or whisper (prayers or spells), to count (one's beads), to repeat (the name of any deity) inaudibly or mentally, to recite (the bead-roll), to make mention of.
more: (in Braj) v. a. See جپنا japnā.

def: s.m. (prov.) = jāpī, jāpak, q.v. s.m. One who mutters prayers and counts his beads, one who recites to himself ( = jāpī).

def: adj. & s.m. Cold, frigid, chilly; motionless, apathetic, senseless, unfeeling; stolid, stupid, idiotic; irrational, inanimate, material; dumb; — a senseless, apathetic, or unfeeling person; a dumb person; an inanimate body, anything void of life; a stupid person, an idiot, a dolt, blockhead
subs: s.m. A dull or stupid person, an idiot
s.m. Any substance that is void of animation

This fleshy frame struck down, the heart's warm blood still struggling hard, A-quivering as in wrestler's grasp, the chattering teeth regard, With calling out wheu .. hoo .. hoo .. hoo and is .. is .. s s .. s .. s .., When upper lower jaws collide, just as the mill grinds grinds, Each tooth like dry peas ground and ground, then winter stern's reign is? jāṛā chaṛhnā , v. n. Caste lost, the stomach empty. (Said sometimes by insincere converts to Christianity). 3. Jāt kī beṭī, jāt ke hī jātī hai . Prov. Daughters of high caste for men of high caste. 4. Shiv kā jāt pāṅti nahīṅ ṭhikainhī, nahīṅ ṭhikainhi kul parivār, he! Tir. This Shiv is without caste or friends, family or name? ( Maina dissuades her daughter, Pārbati , from marrying him). 3. Class; kind; sort; species. 4. A votive offering. jāt birādrī, bhāī birādrī, jāt kuṭumb . See برادري jāt bhāī , n. m. One of the same caste or tribe. jā…

n. m. A cultivator caste of Hindus in Upper India. 1. Ghī Jāt kā, tel hāṭ kā . Prov. Buy your ghī of the Jāt , your oil in the mart. (Pure ghī from the village, pure oil in the bazar where it has settled). 2. Naṭ biddiyā pāī jāë, Jaṭ biddiyā na pāī jāë . Prov. You may fathom the mysteries of the acrobat, but not the wit of the Jāṭ . ☞ Native proverbs ascribe both natural wit and stupidity to the Jaṭ . The story goes that a king having lost his kingdom to a female acrobat on the proviso that no one was found to surpass her, a simple Jāṭ , to the astonishment of all, took up the gauntlet she had thrown and forthwith scrambled up the pole. On reaching the top he discharged a stream of water from his person all round the place. The woman was beaten and the kingdom restored. jaṭ'vā , n. m. 1. A community of jāts. 2. more: E. n. f. H. jāṅt mill. A kind of song sung by women while grinding corn.

def: s.m. Name of a fabulous bird, the king of the vultures (the son of Garuḍ, according to the Rāmāyaṇ); — bdellium.
more: n. prop. Name of a bird, mentioned in the story of Rāmachandra and Rāvaṇa.

def: s.m. Name of a fabulous bird, the king of the vultures (the son of Garuḍ, according to the Rāmāyaṇ); — bdellium.

s.m. The axis or roller (of an oil or sugar mill, which presses the grain or the canes), the upright beam (which moves in the mill); — a post placed in the centre of a tank (to mark its being dedicated to a deity or married to a grove: cf. lāṭh; laṭh).

n. m. S. यष्टि 1. The axis or roller of an oil or sugar mill which presses the grain or the canes; the upright beam which moves in the mill ( lāṭṅ ). 2. (Tir.) A post put up by Hindūs in the centre of a tank to mark its being dedicated to the deity or married to a grove.
s. m. 1. The axis or roller of an oil or sugar-mill which presses the grain or the canes. 2. A post placed by Hindūs in the centre of a tank to mark its being dedicated to the deity.
s.m. A company, band, gang, party, crew (of confederates, &c.), a multitude, mass; a flock; capital, stock; strength
more: n. m. S. यूथ 1. A band; gang. 2. E. A great number; mass; heap; flock. 3. Capital; stock. Jatve jathā hoët tatve na karav, adhik kahāṅ sāṅ karū? Tir. As much as I have so much shall I not spend, from where can I spend more? 5. Strength ( būtā ). jathā bāṅdhnā , v. a. To form a party. jathe-vār , adj. Corporate; joint; common.
adv. S. यथा Correl. of tathā , q. v. In what manner or way; as; like. jathā-shakt ; A. hasb-i-taufīq , According to one's means or income.
s. m. A company, band, gang, party, class. جتها باندهنا jathā bāndhnā, To form a party.
adv. As, according to. جتها جوگيه jathā-jogya, or جتها يوگ jathā-yog ( यथायोग्य ) In a proper manner, suitably, properly.
subs: adj. In a body; corporate, joint, common.

def: adv. (correl. tathā), In which manner, in which way, as, like as, like, according to, comormably to
subs: (S. yathā+artha), adj. & adv. Accordant with reality or fact, conformable to truth, true, exact, actual, real, right, fit, suitable
(or -bādī), adj. & s.m. Speaking truly or fitly; — one who so speaks; a philosopher
jathā-jogyā (S. yathā-yogya), adv. In a proper manner, as it should be, properly, suitably
adv. According to (one's) power or ability, according to (one's) means (syn. ḥasb-ě-taufīq).
